:::{#cor-fubini-rect}

### Continuous functions on a rectangle (corollary of @thm-fubini)

If $f : [a, b] \times [c, d] \to \mathbb{R}$ is **continuous** on the
closed bounded rectangle $[a, b] \times [c, d]$, then:

$$
\ba
\int_a^b \paren{\int_c^d f(x, y)\,dy}\,dx
&= \int_c^d \paren{\int_a^b f(x, y)\,dx}\,dy\\
&= \iint_{[a,b]\times[c,d]} f(x, y)\,dx\,dy.
\ea
$$

[@larsonCalc11e, Theorem 14.2, p. 982]

:::

::: proof

A closed bounded rectangle $[a, b] \times [c, d]$ is both vertically
simple (with $g_1 \equiv c$, $g_2 \equiv d$) and horizontally simple
(with $h_1 \equiv a$, $h_2 \equiv b$).
Applying both parts of @thm-fubini to $f$ on this rectangle gives the
two iterated forms shown.

:::
